info.title: This package is deprecated use FlubuCore.Tool instead. Package was just renamed to FlubuCore.Tool because as of .Net Core 3.0 local and global tools exists.

options.name: --interactive
options.description:
Run's Flubu core in interactive
mode

options.name: --parallel
options.description:
If applied target's are executed
in parallel

options.name: --targetsToExecute
options.description:
Target's that must be executed.
Otherwise fails.

options.name: --configurationFile
options.description:
Path to the Flubu json
configuration file. If not
specified configuration is read
from flubusettings.json

options.name: -ass
options.description:
Directory to search assemblies
to include automatically in
script (Assemblies in
subdirectories are also loaded).
If not specified assemblies are
loaded by default from FlubuLib
directory.

options.name: --nodeps
options.description:
If applied no target
dependencies are executed.

options.name: --dryRun
options.description:
Performs a dry run of the
specified target.

options.name: --noint
options.description:
Disables interactive mode for
all task members. Default values
are used instead.

options.name: --ci
options.description:
Generates configuration file for
specified continous integration
server. Supported values:
Jenkins, AppVeyor, Travis,
AzurePipelines, GithubActions
Flubu internal commands:
<Target> help                                 Shows detailed help for specified target.
new                                           Creates new build script project from template. Execute 'flubu new' for list of templates.
setup                                         Store locations of build script, project file or flubu settings file into '.flubu' file.
You should execute this command at the root of your repository.
Flubu v.6.4.4.0
Script file path was not explicitly specified, searching the default locations.
EXECUTION FAILED:
The build script file was not found.  Please specify location of the build script in the argument '-s={PathToBuildScript}' or run 'flubu setup' to store the location in a file or use some of the default paths for script file:
buildscript.cs
BuildScript.cs
deployscript.cs
DeployScript.cs
DeploymentScript.cs

build/Build.cs

_BuildScript/BuildScript.cs
_BuildScripts/BuildScript.cs

BuildScript/DeploymentScript.cs
